turns.content:
Now I have all the context. Let me build the complete LC-3 assembly program. Key design decisions:

1. Stack region: Use label-based approach (StackMax through StackBase) with R6 as stack pointer
2. Need to add `%` (mod), `@` (XOR), and `neg` operations (neg is triggered by `-`, XOR by `@`, mod by `%`)
3. The main loop from 10.22.asm needs to be extended with `%`, `@`, and `neg` handling
4. Must save/restore R7 around TRAP calls (GETC, OUT, PUTS) since they use TRAP which clobbers R7

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"path": "/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/lab2.asm", "content": ";
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X (exit), C (clear), + (add), * (multiply),
;           % (mod), @ (XOR), neg/- (negate), D (display),
;           digits 0-9 (push integer)
;
; Stack grows from high address (StackBase+1) to low (StackMax).
; R6 = stack pointer, R5 = success(0)/failure(1).
;

        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; Main Program
; ============================================================
        LEA     R6,StackBase    ; Initialize the Stack.
        ADD     R6,R6,#1        ; R6 points one past StackBase (empty)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Main
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Main

; ---- Check the command character in R0 ----
Test
        ; Check for 'X' (exit)
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     Exit

        ; Check for 'C' (clear)
        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        ; Check for '+' (add)
        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpAdd

        ; Check for '*' (multiply)
        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMult

        ; Check for '-' (negate)
        LD      R1,NegMinus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpNeg

        ; Check for '%' (mod)
        LD      R1,NegMod
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMod

        ; Check for '@' (XOR)
        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpXor

        ; Check for 'D' (display)
        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpDisplay

        ; Otherwise must be a digit -- go to PushValue
        BRnzp   PushValue

; ---- Dispatch trampolines (to handle distance limits) ----
DoOpAdd
        JSR     OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMult
        JSR     OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpNeg
        JSR     OpNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMod
        JSR     OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpXor
        JSR     OpXor
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpDisplay
        JSR     OpDisplay
        BRnzp   NewCommand

Exit    HALT

SaveR7Main  .FILL   x0000

PromptMsg   .FILL   x000A
            .STRINGZ "Enter a command:"

NegX        .FILL   xFFA8       ; -'X' = -(x0058)
NegC        .FILL   xFFBD       ; -'C' = -(x0043)
NegPlus     .FILL   xFFD5       ; -'+' = -(x002B)
NegMinus    .FILL   xFFD3       ; -'-' = -(x002D)
NegMult     .FILL   xFFD6       ; -'*' = -(x002A)
NegD        .FILL   xFFBC       ; -'D' = -(x0044)
NegMod      .FILL   xFFDB       ; -'%' = -(x0025)
NegAt       .FILL   xFFC0       ; -'@' = -(x0040)

; ============================================================
; OpClear -- Reset stack pointer
; ============================================================
OpClear
        LEA     R6,StackBase
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        BRnzp   NewCommand

; ============================================================
; OpAdd -- Pop two, add, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpAdd   ST      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpAdd
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = first operand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Add     ; Restore first operand
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; R0 = second + first
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Add
        JSR     PUSH            ; Push result
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
Restore2Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1      ; Put second operand back
Restore1Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1      ; Put first operand back
ExitOpAdd
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
SaveR7OpAdd .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult -- Pop two, multiply, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpMult  ST      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        AND     R3,R3,#0        ; R3 = sign flag
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMult
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = first operand (multiplicand)
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mult
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = second operand (multiplier)
        BRzp    PosMultiplier
        ADD     R3,R3,#1        ; Flag: multiplier is negative
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |multiplier|
PosMultiplier
        AND     R0,R0,#0        ; R0 = product accumulator = 0
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     DoneMultLoop    ; multiplier == 0, product = 0
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; product += multiplicand
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
DoneMultLoop
        ADD     R3,R3,#0        ; Check sign flag
        BRz     NoSignFlipMult
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; Negate product
NoSignFlipMult
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mult
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
Restore2Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMult
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
SaveR7OpMult .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg -- Pop one, negate, push
; ============================================================
OpNeg   ST      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpNeg
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1       ; R0 = -R0
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Neg
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
Restore1Neg
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1     ; Restore the operand
ExitOpNeg
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
SaveR7OpNeg .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod -- Pop two (a then b), compute b % a, range-check, push
;   b % a: remainder when b is divided by a
;   Sign of result follows sign of dividend (b).
; ============================================================
OpMod   ST      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = a (divisor, top of stack)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMod
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = a (divisor)
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = b (dividend)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mod

        ; Save signs and work with absolute values
        AND     R3,R3,#0         ; R3 = sign flag for dividend
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRzp    DividendPos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1         ; dividend is negative
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1         ; R0 = |b|
DividendPos
        ADD     R2,R0,#0         ; R2 = |b| (working dividend)

        ; Make divisor positive
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRzp    DivisorPos
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1         ; R1 = |a|
DivisorPos
        ; Check divisor != 0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     ModDivByZero

        ; Now compute R2 % R1 (both positive)
        ; Subtract R1 from R2 repeatedly
        NOT     R4,R1
        ADD     R4,R4,#1         ; R4 = -|a|
ModLoop
        ADD     R2,R2,R4         ; R2 = R2 - |a|
        BRzp    ModLoop
        ; R2 is now negative, add back once
        ADD     R2,R2,R1         ; R2 = remainder (0 <= R2 < |a|)

        ADD     R0,R2,#0         ; R0 = remainder

        ; Apply sign of dividend
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign
        ; Negate result if dividend was negative
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign        ; if remainder is 0, don't negate
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
ModNoSign
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mod
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

ModDivByZero
        ; Division by zero: print error, restore stack
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        BRnzp   Restore2Mod

Restore2Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMod
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

SaveR7OpMod  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap2  .FILL x0000
DivZeroMsg   .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Divide by Zero."

; ============================================================
; OpXor -- Pop two, XOR, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpXor   ST      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpXor
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = first operand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Xor

        ; XOR R0 and R1 using: a XOR b = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b)
        ; LC-3 has AND and NOT but no OR/XOR directly.
        ; We can compute XOR bit-by-bit, or use the identity:
        ;   a XOR b = NOT(NOT(a AND NOT(b)) AND NOT(NOT(a) AND b))
        ; which is: a XOR b = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b)
        ; OR(x,y) = NOT(AND(NOT(x), NOT(y)))

        ; R0 = a, R1 = b  =>  result = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b)
        NOT     R2,R1            ; R2 = ~b
        AND     R2,R0,R2         ; R2 = a AND ~b

        NOT     R3,R0            ; R3 = ~a
        AND     R3,R3,R1         ; R3 = ~a AND b

        ; OR: R0 = NOT(NOT(R2) AND NOT(R3))
        NOT     R2,R2            ; R2 = ~(a AND ~b)
        NOT     R3,R3            ; R3 = ~(~a AND b)
        AND     R0,R2,R3         ; R0 = ~(a AND ~b) AND ~(~a AND b)
        NOT     R0,R0            ; R0 = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b) = a XOR b

        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Xor
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
Restore2Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpXor
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
SaveR7OpXor .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpDisplay -- Display top of stack (without popping)
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,SaveR7Disp
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpDisplay
        JSR     BinarytoASCII
        ST      R0,SaveR0Disp   ; Save R0 (the value we popped)
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUFF
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LD      R0,SaveR0Disp
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Push displayed value back on stack
ExitOpDisplay
        LD      R7,SaveR7Disp
        RET
SaveR7Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR0Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap3 .FILL x0000
NewlineChar .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck -- Check if R0 is in [-999, 999]
;   Returns R5=0 on success, R5=1 on failure
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,SaveR7RC
        LD      R5,Neg999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 + (-999) = R0 - 999
        BRp     BadRange       ; if R0 > 999
        LD      R5,Pos999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 + 999
        BRn     BadRange       ; if R0 < -999
        AND     R5,R5,#0       ; success
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        RET
BadRange
        LEA     R0,RangeErrorMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1       ; failure
        RET
SaveR7RC     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap4  .FILL x0000
Neg999       .FILL  #-999
Pos999       .FILL  #999
RangeErrorMsg .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Error: Number is out of range."

; ============================================================
; PushValue -- Read digits, convert to binary, push
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,SaveR7PV
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF    ; R1 points to digit buffer
        LD      R2,MaxDigits    ; max 3 digits
ValueLoop
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10      ; Test for newline (x000A = 10)
        BRz     GoodInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     TooLargeInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        BRnzp   ValueLoop

GoodInput
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2        ; R1 = number of characters
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

TooLargeInput
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10     ; check for newline
        BRnp    TooLargeInput
        LEA     R0,TooManyDigits
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7PV     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap5  .FILL x0000
TooManyDigits .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Too many digits"
MaxDigits    .FILL  x0003

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary -- Convert ASCII string to binary
;   R1 = number of digits, ASCIIBUFF holds the digits
;   Result returned in R0
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0        ; R0 = result = 0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     DoneAtoB

        LD      R3,NegASCIIOffset  ; R3 = -x30
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; R2 points to ones digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3        ; Strip ASCII
        ADD     R0,R0,R4        ; Add ones

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; Point to tens digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3        ; Strip ASCII
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4        ; Add tens contribution

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; Point to hundreds digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4        ; Add hundreds contribution

DoneAtoB
        RET

NegASCIIOffset .FILL xFFD0
ASCIIBUFF      .BLKW  4

LookUp10    .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #10
            .FILL  #20
            .FILL  #30
            .FILL  #40
            .FILL  #50
            .FILL  #60
            .FILL  #70
            .FILL  #80
            .FILL  #90

LookUp100   .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #100
            .FILL  #200
            .FILL  #300
            .FILL  #400
            .FILL  #500
            .FILL  #600
            .FILL  #700
            .FILL  #800
            .FILL  #900

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII -- Convert binary (in R0) to ASCII string
;   R0 = signed value in [-999, 999]
;   Output stored in ASCIIBUFF (sign + 3 digits)
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIplus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   Begin100
NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIminus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = |value|
Begin100
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset   ; R2 = x30 ('0')
        LD      R3,Neg100
Loop100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End100
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop100
End100
        STR     R2,R1,#1        ; Store hundreds digit
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3        ; Correct for one-too-many subtracts

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
Begin10
        LD      R3,Neg10
Loop10
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End10
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop10
End10
        STR     R2,R1,#2        ; Store tens digit
        ADD     R0,R0,#10       ; Correct

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0
        STR     R2,R1,#3        ; Store ones digit
        RET

ASCIIplus   .FILL  x002B       ; '+'
ASCIIminus  .FILL  x002D       ; '-'
ASCIIoffset .FILL  x0030       ; '0'
Neg100      .FILL  xFF9C       ; -100
Pos100      .FILL  x0064       ; +100
Neg10       .FILL  xFFF6       ; -10

; ============================================================
; PUSH and POP Subroutines
; ============================================================

; POP: Pop top of stack into R0
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (underflow)
POP
        ST      R1,SaveR1Pop
        LEA     R0,StackBase
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = address just past StackBase
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = -(StackBase+1)
        ADD     R0,R0,R6        ; R0 = R6 - (StackBase+1)
        BRz     Underflow       ; If R6 == StackBase+1, stack is empty
        LDR     R0,R6,#0        ; The actual POP
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; Success
        RET
Underflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; Failure
        RET
SaveR1Pop    .FILL x0000
SaveR7Pop    .FILL x0000
UnderflowMsg .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."

; PUSH: Push R0 onto the stack
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (overflow)
PUSH
        ST      R1,SaveR1Push
        LEA     R1,StackMax
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1        ; R1 = -addr(StackMax)
        ADD     R1,R1,R6        ; R1 = R6 - addr(StackMax)
        BRz     Overflow        ; If R6 == addr(StackMax), full
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        STR     R0,R6,#0        ; The actual PUSH
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; Success
        RET
Overflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Push
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Push
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; Failure
        RET
SaveR1Push   .FILL x0000
SaveR7Push   .FILL x0000
OverflowMsg  .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."

; ============================================================
; Stack Data
; ============================================================
StackMax    .BLKW  10
StackBase   .FILL  x0000

        .END
"}

Now let me also reconsider the `OpMod` implementation. The modulus operation needs careful thought about what "b % a" means with the stack ordering. The stack is LIFO, so if we push 7 then push 3, the top is 3. First POP gives 3 (= a, divisor), second POP gives 7 (= b, dividend). So we compute 7 % 3 = 1. That seems right.

But wait — I need to reconsider the multiplication. In the reference `OpMult`, the range check is done BEFORE applying the sign. Let me look at the reference more carefully:

In 10.14.asm: The multiplicand (R1) keeps its original sign, R2 is the absolute value of multiplier, and the loop adds R1 repeatedly. So if R1 is negative, the product will be negative. The range check is then done on this intermediate result. If the multiplier was negative (R3=1), the result is negated after range check.

Wait, that's actually wrong in the reference if both are negative — if multiplicand is -5 and multiplier is -3, then R1 = -5, R2 = 3, loop gives -5 + -5 + -5 = -15, then since R3=1 we negate to get 15. That's correct.

But the range check happens on -15, which passes (it's in [-999, 999]). Then we negate to 15. That's fine.

However, in my implementation I moved the sign flip BEFORE the range check. Let me fix this to match the reference pattern, doing range check after applying the final sign:

Actually wait, let me re-read my code. I have:
1. Compute product (with sign of multiplicand but absolute multiplier)
2. Apply sign flip if multiplier was negative
3. Range check
4. Push

Actually that's better — we do range check on the FINAL value. The reference does range check before sign flip, which could miss an edge case. My approach is more correct. Let me keep it.

Now let me also think about whether the `OpMod` restore properly restores both values. When we pop a and then b, and need to restore, we need to push b back first (decrement R6), then push a back (decrement R6 again). Since after two POPs the values are still in memory (we just moved R6 past them), decrementing R6 twice restores the original state. That's what `Restore2Mod` / `Restore1Mod` do.

Let me also verify the stack size. `StackMax .BLKW 10` gives 10 slots, and `StackBase .FILL x0000` is one more word. R6 starts at StackBase+1. The stack can hold up to 10 elements (from StackBase down to StackMax). When R6 == addr(StackMax), the stack is full (PUSH checks this). When R6 == StackBase+1, the stack is empty (POP checks this).

Wait, actually the POP check: I compute `R0 = R6 - (StackBase+1)`. If R6 == StackBase+1 (empty), R0 = 0, branch to underflow. Good.

The PUSH check: I compute `R1 = R6 - addr(StackMax)`. If R6 == addr(StackMax) (full, no more room), R1 = 0, branch to overflow. Good.

;
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X (exit), C (clear), + (add), * (multiply),
;           % (mod), @ (XOR), neg/- (negate), D (display),
;           digits 0-9 (push integer)
;
; Stack grows from high address (StackBase+1) to low (StackMax).
; R6 = stack pointer, R5 = success(0)/failure(1).
;

        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; Main Program
; ============================================================
        LEA     R6,StackBase    ; Initialize the Stack.
        ADD     R6,R6,#1        ; R6 points one past StackBase (empty)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Main
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Main

; ---- Check the command character in R0 ----
Test
        ; Check for 'X' (exit)
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     Exit

        ; Check for 'C' (clear)
        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        ; Check for '+' (add)
        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpAdd

        ; Check for '*' (multiply)
        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMult

        ; Check for '-' (negate)
        LD      R1,NegMinus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpNeg

        ; Check for '%' (mod)
        LD      R1,NegMod
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMod

        ; Check for '@' (XOR)
        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpXor

        ; Check for 'D' (display)
        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpDisplay

        ; Otherwise must be a digit -- go to PushValue
        BRnzp   PushValue

; ---- Dispatch trampolines (to handle distance limits) ----
DoOpAdd
        JSR     OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMult
        JSR     OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpNeg
        JSR     OpNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMod
        JSR     OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpXor
        JSR     OpXor
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpDisplay
        JSR     OpDisplay
        BRnzp   NewCommand

Exit    HALT

SaveR7Main  .FILL   x0000

PromptMsg   .FILL   x000A
            .STRINGZ "Enter a command:"

NegX        .FILL   xFFA8       ; -'X' = -(x0058)
NegC        .FILL   xFFBD       ; -'C' = -(x0043)
NegPlus     .FILL   xFFD5       ; -'+' = -(x002B)
NegMinus    .FILL   xFFD3       ; -'-' = -(x002D)
NegMult     .FILL   xFFD6       ; -'*' = -(x002A)
NegD        .FILL   xFFBC       ; -'D' = -(x0044)
NegMod      .FILL   xFFDB       ; -'%' = -(x0025)
NegAt       .FILL   xFFC0       ; -'@' = -(x0040)

; ============================================================
; OpClear -- Reset stack pointer
; ============================================================
OpClear
        LEA     R6,StackBase
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        BRnzp   NewCommand

; ============================================================
; OpAdd -- Pop two, add, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpAdd   ST      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpAdd
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = first operand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Add     ; Restore first operand
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; R0 = second + first
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Add
        JSR     PUSH            ; Push result
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
Restore2Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1      ; Put second operand back
Restore1Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1      ; Put first operand back
ExitOpAdd
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
SaveR7OpAdd .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult -- Pop two, multiply, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpMult  ST      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        AND     R3,R3,#0        ; R3 = sign flag
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMult
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = first operand (multiplicand)
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mult
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = second operand (multiplier)
        BRzp    PosMultiplier
        ADD     R3,R3,#1        ; Flag: multiplier is negative
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |multiplier|
PosMultiplier
        AND     R0,R0,#0        ; R0 = product accumulator = 0
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     DoneMultLoop    ; multiplier == 0, product = 0
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; product += multiplicand
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
DoneMultLoop
        ADD     R3,R3,#0        ; Check sign flag
        BRz     NoSignFlipMult
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; Negate product
NoSignFlipMult
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mult
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
Restore2Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMult
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
SaveR7OpMult .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg -- Pop one, negate, push
; ============================================================
OpNeg   ST      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpNeg
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1       ; R0 = -R0
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Neg
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
Restore1Neg
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1     ; Restore the operand
ExitOpNeg
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
SaveR7OpNeg .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod -- Pop two (a then b), compute b % a, range-check, push
;   b % a: remainder when b is divided by a
;   Sign of result follows sign of dividend (b).
; ============================================================
OpMod   ST      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = a (divisor, top of stack)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMod
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = a (divisor)
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = b (dividend)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mod

        ; Save signs and work with absolute values
        AND     R3,R3,#0         ; R3 = sign flag for dividend
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRzp    DividendPos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1         ; dividend is negative
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1         ; R0 = |b|
DividendPos
        ADD     R2,R0,#0         ; R2 = |b| (working dividend)

        ; Make divisor positive
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRzp    DivisorPos
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1         ; R1 = |a|
DivisorPos
        ; Check divisor != 0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     ModDivByZero

        ; Now compute R2 % R1 (both positive)
        ; Subtract R1 from R2 repeatedly
        NOT     R4,R1
        ADD     R4,R4,#1         ; R4 = -|a|
ModLoop
        ADD     R2,R2,R4         ; R2 = R2 - |a|
        BRzp    ModLoop
        ; R2 is now negative, add back once
        ADD     R2,R2,R1         ; R2 = remainder (0 <= R2 < |a|)

        ADD     R0,R2,#0         ; R0 = remainder

        ; Apply sign of dividend
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign
        ; Negate result if dividend was negative
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign        ; if remainder is 0, don't negate
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
ModNoSign
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mod
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

ModDivByZero
        ; Division by zero: print error, restore stack
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        BRnzp   Restore2Mod

Restore2Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMod
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

SaveR7OpMod  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap2  .FILL x0000
DivZeroMsg   .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Divide by Zero."

; ============================================================
; OpXor -- Pop two, XOR, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpXor   ST      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpXor
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = first operand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Xor

        ; XOR R0 and R1 using: a XOR b = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b)
        ; LC-3 has AND and NOT but no OR/XOR directly.
        ; We can compute XOR bit-by-bit, or use the identity:
        ;   a XOR b = NOT(NOT(a AND NOT(b)) AND NOT(NOT(a) AND b))
        ; which is: a XOR b = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b)
        ; OR(x,y) = NOT(AND(NOT(x), NOT(y)))

        ; R0 = a, R1 = b  =>  result = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b)
        NOT     R2,R1            ; R2 = ~b
        AND     R2,R0,R2         ; R2 = a AND ~b

        NOT     R3,R0            ; R3 = ~a
        AND     R3,R3,R1         ; R3 = ~a AND b

        ; OR: R0 = NOT(NOT(R2) AND NOT(R3))
        NOT     R2,R2            ; R2 = ~(a AND ~b)
        NOT     R3,R3            ; R3 = ~(~a AND b)
        AND     R0,R2,R3         ; R0 = ~(a AND ~b) AND ~(~a AND b)
        NOT     R0,R0            ; R0 = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b) = a XOR b

        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Xor
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
Restore2Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpXor
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
SaveR7OpXor .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpDisplay -- Display top of stack (without popping)
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,SaveR7Disp
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpDisplay
        ST      R0,SaveR0Disp   ; Save R0 (the value we popped)
        JSR     BinarytoASCII
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUFF
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LD      R0,SaveR0Disp
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Push displayed value back on stack
ExitOpDisplay
        LD      R7,SaveR7Disp
        RET
SaveR7Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR0Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap3 .FILL x0000
NewlineChar .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck -- Check if R0 is in [-999, 999]
;   Returns R5=0 on success, R5=1 on failure
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,SaveR7RC
        LD      R5,Neg999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 + (-999) = R0 - 999
        BRp     BadRange       ; if R0 > 999
        LD      R5,Pos999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 + 999
        BRn     BadRange       ; if R0 < -999
        AND     R5,R5,#0       ; success
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        RET
BadRange
        LEA     R0,RangeErrorMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1       ; failure
        RET
SaveR7RC     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap4  .FILL x0000
Neg999       .FILL  #-999
Pos999       .FILL  #999
RangeErrorMsg .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Error: Number is out of range."

; ============================================================
; PushValue -- Read digits, convert to binary, push
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,SaveR7PV
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF    ; R1 points to digit buffer
        LD      R2,MaxDigits    ; max 3 digits
ValueLoop
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10      ; Test for newline (x000A = 10)
        BRz     GoodInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     TooLargeInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        BRnzp   ValueLoop

GoodInput
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2        ; R1 = number of characters
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

TooLargeInput
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10     ; check for newline
        BRnp    TooLargeInput
        LEA     R0,TooManyDigits
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7PV     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap5  .FILL x0000
TooManyDigits .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Too many digits"
MaxDigits    .FILL  x0003

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary -- Convert ASCII string to binary
;   R1 = number of digits, ASCIIBUFF holds the digits
;   Result returned in R0
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0        ; R0 = result = 0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     DoneAtoB

        LD      R3,NegASCIIOffset  ; R3 = -x30
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; R2 points to ones digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3        ; Strip ASCII
        ADD     R0,R0,R4        ; Add ones

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; Point to tens digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3        ; Strip ASCII
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4        ; Add tens contribution

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; Point to hundreds digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4        ; Add hundreds contribution

DoneAtoB
        RET

NegASCIIOffset .FILL xFFD0
ASCIIBUFF      .BLKW  5

LookUp10    .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #10
            .FILL  #20
            .FILL  #30
            .FILL  #40
            .FILL  #50
            .FILL  #60
            .FILL  #70
            .FILL  #80
            .FILL  #90

LookUp100   .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #100
            .FILL  #200
            .FILL  #300
            .FILL  #400
            .FILL  #500
            .FILL  #600
            .FILL  #700
            .FILL  #800
            .FILL  #900

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII -- Convert binary (in R0) to ASCII string
;   R0 = signed value in [-999, 999]
;   Output stored in ASCIIBUFF (sign + 3 digits)
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIplus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   Begin100
NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIminus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = |value|
Begin100
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset   ; R2 = x30 ('0')
        LD      R3,Neg100
Loop100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End100
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop100
End100
        STR     R2,R1,#1        ; Store hundreds digit
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3        ; Correct for one-too-many subtracts

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
Begin10
        LD      R3,Neg10
Loop10
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End10
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop10
End10
        STR     R2,R1,#2        ; Store tens digit
        ADD     R0,R0,#10       ; Correct

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0
        STR     R2,R1,#3        ; Store ones digit
        AND     R2,R2,#0
        STR     R2,R1,#4        ; Null terminator
        RET

ASCIIplus   .FILL  x002B       ; '+'
ASCIIminus  .FILL  x002D       ; '-'
ASCIIoffset .FILL  x0030       ; '0'
Neg100      .FILL  xFF9C       ; -100
Pos100      .FILL  x0064       ; +100
Neg10       .FILL  xFFF6       ; -10

; ============================================================
; PUSH and POP Subroutines
; ============================================================

; POP: Pop top of stack into R0
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (underflow)
POP
        ST      R1,SaveR1Pop
        LEA     R0,StackBase
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = address just past StackBase
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = -(StackBase+1)
        ADD     R0,R0,R6        ; R0 = R6 - (StackBase+1)
        BRz     Underflow       ; If R6 == StackBase+1, stack is empty
        LDR     R0,R6,#0        ; The actual POP
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; Success
        RET
Underflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; Failure
        RET
SaveR1Pop    .FILL x0000
SaveR7Pop    .FILL x0000
UnderflowMsg .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."

; PUSH: Push R0 onto the stack
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (overflow)
PUSH
        ST      R1,SaveR1Push
        LEA     R1,StackMax
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1        ; R1 = -addr(StackMax)
        ADD     R1,R1,R6        ; R1 = R6 - addr(StackMax)
        BRz     Overflow        ; If R6 == addr(StackMax), full
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        STR     R0,R6,#0        ; The actual PUSH
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; Success
        RET
Overflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Push
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Push
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; Failure
        RET
SaveR1Push   .FILL x0000
SaveR7Push   .FILL x0000
OverflowMsg  .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."

; ============================================================
; Stack Data
; ============================================================
StackMax    .BLKW  10
StackBase   .FILL  x0000

        .END

turns.content:
I notice a potential issue with PC-relative offset limits. In LC-3, `LD`, `LEA`, and `ST` have a 9-bit signed offset (±256 words from the current PC). If the program is large enough, labels like `StackBase`, `ASCIIBUFF`, etc. might be out of range from where they're referenced. Let me check some critical distances.

The most concerning references are:
1. `OpClear` references `StackBase` — `OpClear` is relatively early in the code, but `StackBase` is at the very end.
2. `POP` references `StackBase` — POP is near the end, close to StackBase, so that's fine.
3. `PUSH` references `StackMax` — also near the end, fine.

Let me count approximate lines/instructions to see if `OpClear`'s LEA to `StackBase` could be too far. This is a real concern.

tool_results.content:
7:; Stack grows from high address (StackBase+1) to low (StackMax).
16:        LEA     R6,StackBase    ; Initialize the Stack.
17:        ADD     R6,R6,#1        ; R6 points one past StackBase (empty)
37:        BRz     OpClear
109:; OpClear -- Reset stack pointer
111:OpClear
112:        LEA     R6,StackBase
584:        LEA     R0,StackBase
585:        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = address just past StackBase
587:        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = -(StackBase+1)
588:        ADD     R0,R0,R6        ; R0 = R6 - (StackBase+1)
589:        BRz     Underflow       ; If R6 == StackBase+1, stack is empty
613:        LEA     R1,StackMax
615:        ADD     R1,R1,#1        ; R1 = -addr(StackMax)
616:        ADD     R1,R1,R6        ; R1 = R6 - addr(StackMax)
617:        BRz     Overflow        ; If R6 == addr(StackMax), full
640:StackMax    .BLKW  10
641:StackBase   .FILL  x0000

turns.content:
That's a problem. `OpClear` (line ~112) references `StackBase` (line ~641). That's ~530 lines apart, and considering instructions + data, this will far exceed the ±256 word PCoffset9 range for LEA.

Similarly, the initial `LEA R6,StackBase` at line 16 is also way too far from StackBase.

I need to restructure. The best approach is to store the stack base address in a data word near each reference and use `LD` to load it, or place the stack data earlier. Actually, the simplest fix is to store the StackBase address in local data words and use LD (which loads the VALUE at an address, so I'd store the address of StackBase in a .FILL).

Wait, actually the standard approach for the LC-3 calculator is to keep the stack data and references close. Let me restructure by moving the stack to be referenced via stored addresses rather than LEA. I'll use a different approach:

1. Store `StackBase` and `StackMax` addresses in local `.FILL` entries near each use point
2. Or better: put the stack at the beginning and reference it from nearby data words

Actually, the cleanest LC-3 approach is what the reference code (10.24.asm, 10.25.asm) does — define `StackMax` and `StackBase` as labels and use `LEA` from nearby code. But that only works if code is close.

Let me restructure the program to put PUSH/POP and the stack at the top (right after the main dispatch), and use local address-holding `.FILL` words for distant references:

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"content": ";
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X (exit), C (clear), + (add), * (multiply),
;           % (mod), @ (XOR), neg/- (negate), D (display),
;           digits 0-9 (push integer)
;
; Stack grows from high address (StackBase+1) to low (StackMax).
; R6 = stack pointer, R5 = success(0)/failure(1).
;

        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; Main Program
; ============================================================
        LD      R6,StackBaseAddr
        ADD     R6,R6,#1        ; R6 points one past StackBase (empty)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Main
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Main

; ---- Check the command character in R0 ----
Test
        ; Check for 'X' (exit)
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     Exit

        ; Check for 'C' (clear)
        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        ; Check for '+' (add)
        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpAdd

        ; Check for '*' (multiply)
        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMult

        ; Check for '-' (negate)
        LD      R1,NegMinus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpNeg

        ; Check for '%' (mod)
        LD      R1,NegMod
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMod

        ; Check for '@' (XOR)
        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpXor

        ; Check for 'D' (display)
        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpDisplay

        ; Otherwise must be a digit -- go to PushValue
        BRnzp   PushValue

; ---- Dispatch trampolines (to handle distance limits) ----
DoOpAdd
        JSR     OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMult
        JSR     OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpNeg
        JSR     OpNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMod
        JSR     OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpXor
        JSR     OpXor
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpDisplay
        JSR     OpDisplay
        BRnzp   NewCommand

Exit    HALT

; ---- OpClear: reset stack pointer ----
OpClear
        LD      R6,StackBaseAddr
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7Main   .FILL   x0000
StackBaseAddr .FILL  x0000       ; Will be filled with address of StackBase

PromptMsg   .FILL   x000A
            .STRINGZ "Enter a command:"

NegX        .FILL   xFFA8       ; -'X' = -(x0058)
NegC        .FILL   xFFBD       ; -'C' = -(x0043)
NegPlus     .FILL   xFFD5       ; -'+' = -(x002B)
NegMinus    .FILL   xFFD3       ; -'-' = -(x002D)
NegMult     .FILL   xFFD6       ; -'*' = -(x002A)
NegD        .FILL   xFFBC       ; -'D' = -(x0044)
NegMod      .FILL   xFFDB       ; -'%' = -(x0025)
NegAt       .FILL   xFFC0       ; -'@' = -(x0040)

; ============================================================
; OpAdd -- Pop two, add, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpAdd   ST      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpAdd
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = first operand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Add     ; Restore first operand
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; R0 = second + first
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Add
        JSR     PUSH            ; Push result
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
Restore2Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1      ; Put second operand back
Restore1Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1      ; Put first operand back
ExitOpAdd
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
SaveR7OpAdd .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult -- Pop two, multiply, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpMult  ST      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        AND     R3,R3,#0        ; R3 = sign flag
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMult
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = first operand (multiplicand)
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mult
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = second operand (multiplier)
        BRzp    PosMultiplier
        ADD     R3,R3,#1        ; Flag: multiplier is negative
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |multiplier|
PosMultiplier
        AND     R0,R0,#0        ; R0 = product accumulator = 0
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     DoneMultLoop    ; multiplier == 0, product = 0
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; product += multiplicand
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
DoneMultLoop
        ADD     R3,R3,#0        ; Check sign flag
        BRz     NoSignFlipMult
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; Negate product
NoSignFlipMult
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mult
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
Restore2Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMult
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
SaveR7OpMult .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg -- Pop one, negate, push
; ============================================================
OpNeg   ST      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpNeg
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1       ; R0 = -R0
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Neg
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
Restore1Neg
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1     ; Restore the operand
ExitOpNeg
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
SaveR7OpNeg .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod -- Pop two (a then b), compute b % a, range-check, push
;   b % a: remainder when b is divided by a
;   Sign of result follows sign of dividend (b).
; ============================================================
OpMod   ST      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = a (divisor, top of stack)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMod
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = a (divisor)
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = b (dividend)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mod

        ; Save signs and work with absolute values
        AND     R3,R3,#0         ; R3 = sign flag for dividend
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRzp    DividendPos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1         ; dividend is negative
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1         ; R0 = |b|
DividendPos
        ADD     R2,R0,#0         ; R2 = |b| (working dividend)

        ; Make divisor positive
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRzp    DivisorPos
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1         ; R1 = |a|
DivisorPos
        ; Check divisor != 0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     ModDivByZero

        ; Now compute R2 % R1 (both positive)
        ; Subtract R1 from R2 repeatedly
        NOT     R4,R1
        ADD     R4,R4,#1         ; R4 = -|a|
ModLoop
        ADD     R2,R2,R4         ; R2 = R2 - |a|
        BRzp    ModLoop
        ; R2 is now negative, add back once
        ADD     R2,R2,R1         ; R2 = remainder (0 <= R2 < |a|)

        ADD     R0,R2,#0         ; R0 = remainder

        ; Apply sign of dividend
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign
        ; Negate result if dividend was negative
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign        ; if remainder is 0, don't negate
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
ModNoSign
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mod
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

ModDivByZero
        ; Division by zero: print error, restore stack
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        BRnzp   Restore2Mod

Restore2Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMod
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

SaveR7OpMod  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap2  .FILL x0000
DivZeroMsg   .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Divide by Zero."

; ============================================================
; OpXor -- Pop two, XOR, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpXor   ST      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpXor
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = first operand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Xor

        ; XOR R0 and R1: a XOR b = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b)
        ; OR(x,y) = NOT(NOT(x) AND NOT(y))
        NOT     R2,R1            ; R2 = ~b
        AND     R2,R0,R2         ; R2 = a AND ~b

        NOT     R3,R0            ; R3 = ~a
        AND     R3,R3,R1         ; R3 = ~a AND b

        NOT     R2,R2            ; R2 = ~(a AND ~b)
        NOT     R3,R3            ; R3 = ~(~a AND b)
        AND     R0,R2,R3         ; R0 = ~(a AND ~b) AND ~(~a AND b)
        NOT     R0,R0            ; R0 = a XOR b

        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Xor
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
Restore2Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpXor
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
SaveR7OpXor .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpDisplay -- Display top of stack (without popping)
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,SaveR7Disp
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpDisplay
        ST      R0,SaveR0Disp   ; Save the value we popped
        JSR     BinarytoASCII
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUF2
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LD      R0,SaveR0Disp
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Push displayed value back on stack
        STR     R0,R6,#0        ; Store it back
ExitOpDisplay
        LD      R7,SaveR7Disp
        RET
SaveR7Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR0Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap3 .FILL x0000
NewlineChar .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck -- Check if R0 is in [-999, 999]
;   Returns R5=0 on success, R5=1 on failure
;   Preserves R0 on failure (for caller to handle restore)
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,SaveR7RC
        ST      R0,SaveR0RC     ; Save R0 in case of failure
        LD      R5,Neg999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 + (-999) = R0 - 999
        BRp     BadRange       ; if R0 > 999
        LD      R5,Pos999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 + 999
        BRn     BadRange       ; if R0 < -999
        AND     R5,R5,#0       ; success
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        RET
BadRange
        LEA     R0,RangeErrorMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        LD      R0,SaveR0RC     ; Restore R0
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1       ; failure
        RET
SaveR7RC     .FILL x0000
SaveR0RC     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap4  .FILL x0000
Neg999       .FILL  #-999
Pos999       .FILL  #999
RangeErrorMsg .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Error: Number is out of range."

; ============================================================
; PushValue -- Read digits, convert to binary, push
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,SaveR7PV
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUF2    ; R1 points to digit buffer
        LD      R2,MaxDigits    ; max 3 digits
ValueLoop
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10      ; Test for newline (x000A = 10)
        BRz     GoodInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     TooLargeInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        BRnzp   ValueLoop

GoodInput
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUF2
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2        ; R1 = number of characters
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

TooLargeInput
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10     ; check for newline
        BRnp    TooLargeInput
        LEA     R0,TooManyDigits
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7PV     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap5  .FILL x0000
TooManyDigits .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Too many digits"
MaxDigits    .FILL  x0003

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary -- Convert ASCII string to binary
;   R1 = number of digits, ASCIIBUF2 holds the digits
;   Result returned in R0
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0        ; R0 = result = 0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     DoneAtoB

        LD      R3,NegASCIIOffset  ; R3 = -x30
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUF2
        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; R2 points to ones digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3        ; Strip ASCII
        ADD     R0,R0,R4        ; Add ones

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; Point to tens digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3        ; Strip ASCII
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4        ; Add tens contribution

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; Point to hundreds digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4        ; Add hundreds contribution

DoneAtoB
        RET

NegASCIIOffset .FILL xFFD0
ASCIIBUF2      .BLKW  5

LookUp10    .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #10
            .FILL  #20
            .FILL  #30
            .FILL  #40
            .FILL  #50
            .FILL  #60
            .FILL  #70
            .FILL  #80
            .FILL  #90

LookUp100   .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #100
            .FILL  #200
            .FILL  #300
            .FILL  #400
            .FILL  #500
            .FILL  #600
            .FILL  #700
            .FILL  #800
            .FILL  #900

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII -- Convert binary (in R0) to ASCII string
;   R0 = signed value in [-999, 999]
;   Output stored in ASCIIBUF2 (sign + 3 digits + null)
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUF2
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIplus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   Begin100
NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIminus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = |value|
Begin100
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset   ; R2 = x30 ('0')
        LD      R3,Neg100
Loop100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End100
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop100
End100
        STR     R2,R1,#1        ; Store hundreds digit
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3        ; Correct for one-too-many subtracts

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        LD      R3,Neg10
Loop10
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End10
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop10
End10
        STR     R2,R1,#2        ; Store tens digit
        ADD     R0,R0,#10       ; Correct

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0
        STR     R2,R1,#3        ; Store ones digit
        AND     R2,R2,#0
        STR     R2,R1,#4        ; Null terminator
        RET

ASCIIplus   .FILL  x002B       ; '+'
ASCIIminus  .FILL  x002D       ; '-'
ASCIIoffset .FILL  x0030       ; '0'
Neg100      .FILL  xFF9C       ; -100
Pos100      .FILL  x0064       ; +100
Neg10       .FILL  xFFF6       ; -10

; ============================================================
; PUSH and POP Subroutines
; ============================================================

; POP: Pop top of stack into R0
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (underflow)
POP
        ST      R1,SaveR1Pop
        LEA     R0,StackBase
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = address of (StackBase+1)
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = -(StackBase+1)
        ADD     R0,R0,R6        ; R0 = R6 - (StackBase+1)
        BRz     Underflow       ; If R6 == StackBase+1, stack is empty
        LDR     R0,R6,#0        ; The actual POP
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; Success
        RET
Underflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; Failure
        RET
SaveR1Pop    .FILL x0000
SaveR7Pop    .FILL x0000
UnderflowMsg .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."

; PUSH: Push R0 onto the stack
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (overflow)
PUSH
        ST      R1,SaveR1Push
        LEA     R1,StackMax
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1        ; R1 = -addr(StackMax)
        ADD     R1,R1,R6        ; R1 = R6 - addr(StackMax)
        BRz     Overflow        ; If R6 == addr(StackMax), full
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        STR     R0,R6,#0        ; The actual PUSH
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; Success
        RET
Overflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Push
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Push
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; Failure
        RET
SaveR1Push   .FILL x0000
SaveR7Push   .FILL x0000
OverflowMsg  .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."

; ============================================================
; Stack Data  (must be near PUSH/POP for LEA to reach)
; ============================================================
StackMax    .BLKW  10
StackBase   .FILL  x0000

        .END
", "path": "/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/lab2.asm"}

turns.content:
Now I still have the issue with `StackBaseAddr` at the top needing to contain the actual address of `StackBase`. Since `StackBase` is a label, I can't use `.FILL StackBase` in standard LC-3 assemblers (some do support it, some don't). 

Let me reconsider the approach. The standard LC-3 textbook approach (as in 10.5.asm) stores `-BASE` as a constant. Let me use that pattern: store the negative of the base/max addresses and use LD + ADD.

Actually, looking at the reference 10.5.asm more carefully:
- `BASE .FILL xC001` — this is `-x3FFF` (the negative of the base address)
- `MAX .FILL xC005` — this is `-x3FFB`

The reference uses fixed addresses (x3FFF = BASE, x3FFB = MAX). But 10.24/10.25 use LEA-relative addresses with labels. Since we have a large program, let me use the fixed-address approach (x3FFF/x3FFB like the textbook), which avoids LEA range issues entirely.

Wait, that won't work either because our program + data already occupies addresses starting at x3000 and extends quite far. The stack at x3FFB-x3FFF might overlap with our code.

Let me count how many words our program occupies. Let me estimate:
- From x3000, the program is roughly 400+ instructions/data words
- x3000 + 400 = x3190 or so
- x3FFB would be well clear

Actually, let me just check by trying to assemble. But first, let me use the fixed stack address approach which is cleaner:

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"content": ";
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X (exit), C (clear), + (add), * (multiply),
;           % (mod), @ (XOR), neg/- (negate), D (display),
;           digits 0-9 (push integer)
;
; Stack: fixed addresses x3FFF (BASE) down to x3FF6 (MAX), 10 slots.
; R6 = stack pointer (points to top element).
; R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure.
;

        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; Main Program
; ============================================================
        LD      R6,StackBase     ; R6 = x4000 (one past base, empty)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Main
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Main

; ---- Check the command character in R0 ----
Test
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     Exit

        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpAdd

        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMult

        LD      R1,NegMinus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpNeg

        LD      R1,NegMod
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMod

        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpXor

        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpDisplay

        ; Otherwise must be a digit
        BRnzp   PushValue

; ---- Dispatch trampolines ----
DoOpAdd
        JSR     OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMult
        JSR     OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpNeg
        JSR     OpNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMod
        JSR     OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpXor
        JSR     OpXor
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpDisplay
        JSR     OpDisplay
        BRnzp   NewCommand

Exit    HALT

; ---- OpClear: reset stack pointer ----
OpClear
        LD      R6,StackBase
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7Main   .FILL  x0000
StackBase    .FILL  x4000       ; Empty stack: R6 points here (one past x3FFF)

PromptMsg   .FILL   x000A
            .STRINGZ "Enter a command:"

NegX        .FILL   xFFA8       ; -'X'
NegC        .FILL   xFFBD       ; -'C'
NegPlus     .FILL   xFFD5       ; -'+'
NegMinus    .FILL   xFFD3       ; -'-'
NegMult     .FILL   xFFD6       ; -'*'
NegD        .FILL   xFFBC       ; -'D'
NegMod      .FILL   xFFDB       ; -'%'
NegAt       .FILL   xFFC0       ; -'@'

; ============================================================
; OpAdd -- Pop two, add, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpAdd   ST      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpAdd
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = first operand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Add
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; R0 = second + first
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Add
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
Restore2Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpAdd
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
SaveR7OpAdd .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult -- Pop two, multiply, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpMult  ST      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        AND     R3,R3,#0        ; R3 = sign flag
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMult
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = multiplicand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mult
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = multiplier
        BRzp    PosMultiplier
        ADD     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |multiplier|
PosMultiplier
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     DoneMultLoop
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
DoneMultLoop
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     NoSignFlipMult
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
NoSignFlipMult
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mult
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
Restore2Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMult
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
SaveR7OpMult .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg -- Pop one, negate, push
; ============================================================
OpNeg   ST      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpNeg
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Neg
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
Restore1Neg
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpNeg
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
SaveR7OpNeg .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod -- Pop two (a then b), compute b % a, push
; ============================================================
OpMod   ST      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = a (divisor)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMod
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = b (dividend)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mod

        AND     R3,R3,#0         ; R3 = sign flag for dividend
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRzp    DividendPos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1         ; R0 = |b|
DividendPos
        ADD     R2,R0,#0         ; R2 = |b|

        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRzp    DivisorPos
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1         ; R1 = |a|
DivisorPos
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     ModDivByZero

        NOT     R4,R1
        ADD     R4,R4,#1         ; R4 = -|a|
ModLoop
        ADD     R2,R2,R4
        BRzp    ModLoop
        ADD     R2,R2,R1         ; remainder

        ADD     R0,R2,#0
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
ModNoSign
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mod
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

ModDivByZero
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        BRnzp   Restore2Mod

Restore2Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMod
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

SaveR7OpMod  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap2  .FILL x0000
DivZeroMsg   .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Divide by Zero."

; ============================================================
; OpXor -- Pop two, XOR, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpXor   ST      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpXor
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = first
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Xor

        ; a XOR b = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b)
        NOT     R2,R1            ; ~b
        AND     R2,R0,R2         ; a AND ~b

        NOT     R3,R0            ; ~a
        AND     R3,R3,R1         ; ~a AND b

        ; OR via De Morgan: NOT(NOT(x) AND NOT(y))
        NOT     R2,R2
        NOT     R3,R3
        AND     R0,R2,R3
        NOT     R0,R0            ; a XOR b

        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Xor
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
Restore2Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpXor
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
SaveR7OpXor .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpDisplay -- Display top of stack (without popping)
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,SaveR7Disp
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpDisplay
        ST      R0,SaveR0Disp   ; Save value before BinarytoASCII
        JSR     BinarytoASCII
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUF2
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LD      R0,SaveR0Disp
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Push displayed value back
        STR     R0,R6,#0
ExitOpDisplay
        LD      R7,SaveR7Disp
        RET
SaveR7Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR0Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap3 .FILL x0000
NewlineChar .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck -- Check if R0 is in [-999, 999]
;   R5=0 success, R5=1 failure. Preserves R0.
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,SaveR7RC
        ST      R0,SaveR0RC
        LD      R5,Neg999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 - 999
        BRp     BadRange
        LD      R5,Pos999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 + 999
        BRn     BadRange
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        RET
BadRange
        LEA     R0,RangeErrorMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        LD      R0,SaveR0RC
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
SaveR7RC     .FILL x0000
SaveR0RC     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap4  .FILL x0000
Neg999       .FILL  #-999
Pos999       .FILL  #999
RangeErrorMsg .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Error: Number is out of range."

; ============================================================
; PushValue -- Read digits, convert to binary, push
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,SaveR7PV
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUF2
        LD      R2,MaxDigits
ValueLoop
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10      ; Test for newline
        BRz     GoodInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     TooLargeInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        BRnzp   ValueLoop

GoodInput
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUF2
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2        ; R1 = number of characters
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

TooLargeInput
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10
        BRnp    TooLargeInput
        LEA     R0,TooManyDigits
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7PV     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap5  .FILL x0000
TooManyDigits .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Too many digits"
MaxDigits    .FILL  x0003

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary -- Convert ASCII string to binary
;   R1 = number of digits, ASCIIBUF2 holds the digits
;   Result returned in R0
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     DoneAtoB

        LD      R3,NegASCIIOffset
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUF2
        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; ones digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        ADD     R0,R0,R4

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; tens digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; hundreds digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4

DoneAtoB
        RET

NegASCIIOffset .FILL xFFD0
ASCIIBUF2      .BLKW  5

LookUp10    .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #10
            .FILL  #20
            .FILL  #30
            .FILL  #40
            .FILL  #50
            .FILL  #60
            .FILL  #70
            .FILL  #80
            .FILL  #90

LookUp100   .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #100
            .FILL  #200
            .FILL  #300
            .FILL  #400
            .FILL  #500
            .FILL  #600
            .FILL  #700
            .FILL  #800
            .FILL  #900

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII -- Convert binary (R0) to ASCII string
;   R0 = signed value in [-999, 999]
;   Output stored in ASCIIBUF2 (sign + 3 digits + null)
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUF2
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIplus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   Begin100
NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIminus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
Begin100
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        LD      R3,Neg100
Loop100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End100
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop100
End100
        STR     R2,R1,#1
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        LD      R3,Neg10
Loop10
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End10
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop10
End10
        STR     R2,R1,#2
        ADD     R0,R0,#10

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0
        STR     R2,R1,#3
        AND     R2,R2,#0
        STR     R2,R1,#4        ; Null terminator
        RET

ASCIIplus   .FILL  x002B
ASCIIminus  .FILL  x002D
ASCIIoffset .FILL  x0030
Neg100      .FILL  xFF9C
Pos100      .FILL  x0064
Neg10       .FILL  xFFF6

; ============================================================
; PUSH and POP Subroutines (using fixed stack addresses)
; ============================================================

; POP: Pop top of stack into R0
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (underflow)
POP
        ST      R1,SaveR1Pop
        LD      R1,NegStackBase  ; R1 = -x4000
        ADD     R1,R6,R1         ; R1 = R6 - x4000
        BRz     Underflow
        LDR     R0,R6,#0
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
Underflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
SaveR1Pop    .FILL x0000
SaveR7Pop    .FILL x0000
NegStackBase .FILL xC000       ; -x4000
UnderflowMsg .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."

; PUSH: Push R0 onto the stack
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (overflow)
PUSH
        ST      R1,SaveR1Push
        LD      R1,NegStackMax   ; R1 = -x3FF6
        ADD     R1,R6,R1         ; R1 = R6 - x3FF6
        BRz     Overflow
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        STR     R0,R6,#0
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
Overflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Push
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Push
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
SaveR1Push   .FILL x0000
SaveR7Push   .FILL x0000
NegStackMax  .FILL xC00A       ; -x3FF6
OverflowMsg  .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."

        .END
