turns.content:
Now let me verify the fixed stack addresses:
- Stack BASE = x3FFF, so empty stack has R6 = x4000 (one past base)
- Stack MAX = x3FF6 (10 slots: x3FFF, x3FFE, x3FFD, x3FFC, x3FFB, x3FFA, x3FF9, x3FF8, x3FF7, x3FF6)
- POP underflow: R6 == x4000 means empty → `-x4000 = xC000`
- PUSH overflow: R6 == x3FF6 means full → `-x3FF6 = xC00A`

Let me verify: `x3FF6` in binary is `0011 1111 1111 0110`. Two's complement negation: `1100 0000 0000 1010` = `xC00A`. ✓

And `x4000` negation: `1100 0000 0000 0000` = `xC000`. ✓

Now let me also verify `StackBase .FILL x4000` — when we do `LD R6,StackBase`, R6 gets x4000. Then the stack grows down from x3FFF.

turns.content:
OK, let me think about the restructuring more holistically. The issue is:
1. Main dispatch → PushValue: too far (307 words with BRnzp PCoffset9)
2. PushValue → NewCommand: too far back (-364, -377)

The best solution: Move PushValue code right after the dispatch trampolines (before OpAdd). Then the `BRnzp NewCommand` from PushValue will be close enough, and the `BRnzp PushValue` from the dispatch will be close too.

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"content": ";
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X (exit), C (clear), + (add), * (multiply),
;           % (mod), @ (XOR), neg/- (negate), D (display),
;           digits 0-9 (push integer)
;
; Stack: fixed addresses x3FFF (BASE) down to x3FF6 (MAX), 10 slots.
; R6 = stack pointer (points to top element, starts at x4000 = empty).
; R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure.
;

        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; Main Program
; ============================================================
        LD      R6,StackBaseVal  ; R6 = x4000 (empty stack)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Main
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Main

; ---- Check the command character in R0 ----
Test
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     Exit

        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpAdd

        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMult

        LD      R1,NegMinus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpNeg

        LD      R1,NegMod
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMod

        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpXor

        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpDisplay

        ; Otherwise must be a digit
        BRnzp   PushValue

; ---- Dispatch trampolines ----
DoOpAdd
        JSR     OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMult
        JSR     OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpNeg
        JSR     OpNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMod
        JSR     OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpXor
        JSR     OpXor
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpDisplay
        JSR     OpDisplay
        BRnzp   NewCommand

Exit    HALT

; ---- OpClear: reset stack pointer ----
OpClear
        LD      R6,StackBaseVal
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7Main   .FILL  x0000
StackBaseVal .FILL  x4000       ; Empty stack pointer value

PromptMsg   .FILL   x000A
            .STRINGZ "Enter a command:"

NegX        .FILL   xFFA8       ; -'X'
NegC        .FILL   xFFBD       ; -'C'
NegPlus     .FILL   xFFD5       ; -'+'
NegMinus    .FILL   xFFD3       ; -'-'
NegMult     .FILL   xFFD6       ; -'*'
NegD        .FILL   xFFBC       ; -'D'
NegMod      .FILL   xFFDB       ; -'%'
NegAt       .FILL   xFFC0       ; -'@'

; ============================================================
; PushValue -- Read digits, convert to binary, push
;   (Placed here so BRnzp from main dispatch and back are in range)
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,SaveR7PV
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUF2
        LD      R2,MaxDigits
ValueLoop
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10      ; Test for newline
        BRz     GoodInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     TooLargeInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        BRnzp   ValueLoop

GoodInput
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUF2
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2        ; R1 = number of characters
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

TooLargeInput
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10
        BRnp    TooLargeInput
        LEA     R0,TooManyDigits
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7PV     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap5  .FILL x0000
TooManyDigits .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Too many digits"
MaxDigits    .FILL  x0003

; ============================================================
; OpAdd -- Pop two, add, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpAdd   ST      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpAdd
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = first operand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Add
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; R0 = second + first
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Add
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
Restore2Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpAdd
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
SaveR7OpAdd .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult -- Pop two, multiply, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpMult  ST      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        AND     R3,R3,#0        ; R3 = sign flag
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMult
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = multiplicand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mult
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = multiplier
        BRzp    PosMultiplier
        ADD     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |multiplier|
PosMultiplier
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     DoneMultLoop
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
DoneMultLoop
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     NoSignFlipMult
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
NoSignFlipMult
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mult
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
Restore2Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMult
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
SaveR7OpMult .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg -- Pop one, negate, push
; ============================================================
OpNeg   ST      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpNeg
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Neg
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
Restore1Neg
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpNeg
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
SaveR7OpNeg .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod -- Pop two (a then b), compute b % a, push
; ============================================================
OpMod   ST      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = a (divisor)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMod
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = b (dividend)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mod

        AND     R3,R3,#0         ; R3 = sign flag for dividend
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRzp    DividendPos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1         ; R0 = |b|
DividendPos
        ADD     R2,R0,#0         ; R2 = |b|

        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRzp    DivisorPos
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1         ; R1 = |a|
DivisorPos
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     ModDivByZero

        NOT     R4,R1
        ADD     R4,R4,#1         ; R4 = -|a|
ModLoop
        ADD     R2,R2,R4
        BRzp    ModLoop
        ADD     R2,R2,R1         ; remainder

        ADD     R0,R2,#0
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
ModNoSign
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mod
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

ModDivByZero
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        BRnzp   Restore2Mod

Restore2Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMod
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

SaveR7OpMod  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap2  .FILL x0000
DivZeroMsg   .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Divide by Zero."

; ============================================================
; OpXor -- Pop two, XOR, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpXor   ST      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpXor
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = first
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Xor

        ; a XOR b = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b)
        NOT     R2,R1            ; ~b
        AND     R2,R0,R2         ; a AND ~b
        NOT     R3,R0            ; ~a
        AND     R3,R3,R1         ; ~a AND b
        ; OR via De Morgan
        NOT     R2,R2
        NOT     R3,R3
        AND     R0,R2,R3
        NOT     R0,R0            ; a XOR b

        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Xor
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
Restore2Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpXor
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
SaveR7OpXor .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpDisplay -- Display top of stack (without popping)
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,SaveR7Disp
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpDisplay
        ST      R0,SaveR0Disp   ; Save value
        JSR     BinarytoASCII
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUF2
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LD      R0,SaveR0Disp
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Push displayed value back
        STR     R0,R6,#0
ExitOpDisplay
        LD      R7,SaveR7Disp
        RET
SaveR7Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR0Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap3 .FILL x0000
NewlineChar .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck -- Check if R0 is in [-999, 999]
;   R5=0 success, R5=1 failure. Preserves R0.
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,SaveR7RC
        ST      R0,SaveR0RC
        LD      R5,Neg999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 - 999
        BRp     BadRange
        LD      R5,Pos999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 + 999
        BRn     BadRange
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        RET
BadRange
        LEA     R0,RangeErrorMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        LD      R0,SaveR0RC
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
SaveR7RC     .FILL x0000
SaveR0RC     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap4  .FILL x0000
Neg999       .FILL  #-999
Pos999       .FILL  #999
RangeErrorMsg .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Error: Number is out of range."

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary -- Convert ASCII string to binary
;   R1 = number of digits, ASCIIBUF2 holds the digits
;   Result returned in R0
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     DoneAtoB

        LD      R3,NegASCIIOffset
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUF2
        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; ones digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        ADD     R0,R0,R4

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; tens digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; hundreds digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4

DoneAtoB
        RET

NegASCIIOffset .FILL xFFD0
ASCIIBUF2      .BLKW  5

LookUp10    .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #10
            .FILL  #20
            .FILL  #30
            .FILL  #40
            .FILL  #50
            .FILL  #60
            .FILL  #70
            .FILL  #80
            .FILL  #90

LookUp100   .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #100
            .FILL  #200
            .FILL  #300
            .FILL  #400
            .FILL  #500
            .FILL  #600
            .FILL  #700
            .FILL  #800
            .FILL  #900

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII -- Convert binary (R0) to ASCII string
;   R0 = signed value in [-999, 999]
;   Output stored in ASCIIBUF2 (sign + 3 digits + null)
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUF2
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIplus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   Begin100
NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIminus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
Begin100
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        LD      R3,Neg100
Loop100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End100
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop100
End100
        STR     R2,R1,#1
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        LD      R3,Neg10
Loop10
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End10
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop10
End10
        STR     R2,R1,#2
        ADD     R0,R0,#10

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0
        STR     R2,R1,#3
        AND     R2,R2,#0
        STR     R2,R1,#4        ; Null terminator
        RET

ASCIIplus   .FILL  x002B
ASCIIminus  .FILL  x002D
ASCIIoffset .FILL  x0030
Neg100      .FILL  xFF9C
Pos100      .FILL  x0064
Neg10       .FILL  xFFF6

; ============================================================
; PUSH and POP Subroutines (using fixed stack addresses)
; ============================================================

; POP: Pop top of stack into R0
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (underflow)
POP
        ST      R1,SaveR1Pop
        LD      R1,NegSBase      ; R1 = -x4000
        ADD     R1,R6,R1         ; R1 = R6 - x4000
        BRz     Underflow
        LDR     R0,R6,#0
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
Underflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
SaveR1Pop    .FILL x0000
SaveR7Pop    .FILL x0000
NegSBase     .FILL xC000       ; -x4000
UnderflowMsg .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."

; PUSH: Push R0 onto the stack
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (overflow)
PUSH
        ST      R1,SaveR1Push
        LD      R1,NegSMax       ; R1 = -x3FF6
        ADD     R1,R6,R1         ; R1 = R6 - x3FF6
        BRz     Overflow
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        STR     R0,R6,#0
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
Overflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Push
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Push
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
SaveR1Push   .FILL x0000
SaveR7Push   .FILL x0000
NegSMax      .FILL xC00A       ; -x3FF6
OverflowMsg  .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."

        .END

turns.content: Still two LEA offset issues — PushValue at the beginning references ASCIIBUF2 which is far away. I need ASCIIBUF2 to be near PushValue, or use a different method. Let me put the buffer and related data near PushValue:

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"content": ";
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X (exit), C (clear), + (add), * (multiply),
;           % (mod), @ (XOR), neg/- (negate), D (display),
;           digits 0-9 (push integer)
;
; Stack: fixed addresses x3FFF (BASE) down to x3FF6 (MAX), 10 slots.
; R6 = stack pointer (points to top element, starts at x4000 = empty).
; R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure.
;

        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; Main Program
; ============================================================
        LD      R6,StackBaseVal  ; R6 = x4000 (empty stack)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Main
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Main

; ---- Check the command character in R0 ----
Test
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     Exit

        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpAdd

        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMult

        LD      R1,NegMinus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpNeg

        LD      R1,NegMod
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMod

        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpXor

        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpDisplay

        ; Otherwise must be a digit
        BRnzp   PushValue

; ---- Dispatch trampolines ----
DoOpAdd
        JSR     OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMult
        JSR     OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpNeg
        JSR     OpNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMod
        JSR     OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpXor
        JSR     OpXor
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpDisplay
        JSR     OpDisplay
        BRnzp   NewCommand

Exit    HALT

; ---- OpClear: reset stack pointer ----
OpClear
        LD      R6,StackBaseVal
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7Main   .FILL  x0000
StackBaseVal .FILL  x4000       ; Empty stack pointer value

PromptMsg   .FILL   x000A
            .STRINGZ "Enter a command:"

NegX        .FILL   xFFA8       ; -'X'
NegC        .FILL   xFFBD       ; -'C'
NegPlus     .FILL   xFFD5       ; -'+'
NegMinus    .FILL   xFFD3       ; -'-'
NegMult     .FILL   xFFD6       ; -'*'
NegD        .FILL   xFFBC       ; -'D'
NegMod      .FILL   xFFDB       ; -'%'
NegAt       .FILL   xFFC0       ; -'@'

; ============================================================
; PushValue -- Read digits, convert to binary, push
;   (Placed near main dispatch so BRnzp NewCommand is in range)
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,SaveR7PV
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUF     ; R1 points to digit buffer
        LD      R2,MaxDigits
ValueLoop
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10      ; Test for newline
        BRz     GoodInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     TooLargeInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        BRnzp   ValueLoop

GoodInput
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUF
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2        ; R1 = number of characters
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

TooLargeInput
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10
        BRnp    TooLargeInput
        LEA     R0,TooManyDigits
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7PV     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap5  .FILL x0000
TooManyDigits .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Too many digits"
MaxDigits    .FILL  x0003
; ---- ASCIIBUF placed here near PushValue/ASCIItoBinary/BinarytoASCII ----
ASCIIBUF     .BLKW  5

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary -- Convert ASCII string to binary
;   R1 = number of digits, ASCIIBUF holds the digits
;   Result returned in R0
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     DoneAtoB

        LD      R3,NegASCIIOffset
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUF
        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; ones digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        ADD     R0,R0,R4

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; tens digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; hundreds digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4

DoneAtoB
        RET

NegASCIIOffset .FILL xFFD0

LookUp10    .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #10
            .FILL  #20
            .FILL  #30
            .FILL  #40
            .FILL  #50
            .FILL  #60
            .FILL  #70
            .FILL  #80
            .FILL  #90

LookUp100   .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #100
            .FILL  #200
            .FILL  #300
            .FILL  #400
            .FILL  #500
            .FILL  #600
            .FILL  #700
            .FILL  #800
            .FILL  #900

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII -- Convert binary (R0) to ASCII string
;   R0 = signed value in [-999, 999]
;   Output stored in ASCIIBUF (sign + 3 digits + null)
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUF
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIplus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   Begin100
NegSign
        LD      R2,ASCIIminus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
Begin100
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        LD      R3,Neg100
Loop100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End100
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop100
End100
        STR     R2,R1,#1
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        LD      R3,Neg10
Loop10
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     End10
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   Loop10
End10
        STR     R2,R1,#2
        ADD     R0,R0,#10

        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0
        STR     R2,R1,#3
        AND     R2,R2,#0
        STR     R2,R1,#4        ; Null terminator
        RET

ASCIIplus   .FILL  x002B
ASCIIminus  .FILL  x002D
ASCIIoffset .FILL  x0030
Neg100      .FILL  xFF9C
Pos100      .FILL  x0064
Neg10       .FILL  xFFF6

; ============================================================
; OpAdd -- Pop two, add, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpAdd   ST      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpAdd
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = first operand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Add
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; R0 = second + first
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Add
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
Restore2Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Add
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpAdd
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        RET
SaveR7OpAdd .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult -- Pop two, multiply, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpMult  ST      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        AND     R3,R3,#0        ; R3 = sign flag
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMult
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = multiplicand
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mult
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = multiplier
        BRzp    PosMultiplier
        ADD     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |multiplier|
PosMultiplier
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     DoneMultLoop
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
DoneMultLoop
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     NoSignFlipMult
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
NoSignFlipMult
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mult
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
Restore2Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mult
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMult
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        RET
SaveR7OpMult .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg -- Pop one, negate, push
; ============================================================
OpNeg   ST      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpNeg
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Neg
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
Restore1Neg
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpNeg
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        RET
SaveR7OpNeg .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod -- Pop two (a then b), compute b % a, push
; ============================================================
OpMod   ST      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = a (divisor)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpMod
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP              ; R0 = b (dividend)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Mod

        AND     R3,R3,#0         ; R3 = sign flag for dividend
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRzp    DividendPos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1         ; R0 = |b|
DividendPos
        ADD     R2,R0,#0         ; R2 = |b|

        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRzp    DivisorPos
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1         ; R1 = |a|
DivisorPos
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     ModDivByZero

        NOT     R4,R1
        ADD     R4,R4,#1         ; R4 = -|a|
ModLoop
        ADD     R2,R2,R4
        BRzp    ModLoop
        ADD     R2,R2,R1         ; remainder

        ADD     R0,R2,#0
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRz     ModNoSign
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
ModNoSign
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Mod
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

ModDivByZero
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap2
        BRnzp   Restore2Mod

Restore2Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Mod
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpMod
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        RET

SaveR7OpMod  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap2  .FILL x0000
DivZeroMsg   .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Divide by Zero."

; ============================================================
; OpXor -- Pop two, XOR, range-check, push
; ============================================================
OpXor   ST      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpXor
        ADD     R1,R0,#0         ; R1 = first
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore1Xor

        ; a XOR b = (a AND ~b) OR (~a AND b)
        NOT     R2,R1            ; ~b
        AND     R2,R0,R2         ; a AND ~b
        NOT     R3,R0            ; ~a
        AND     R3,R3,R1         ; ~a AND b
        ; OR via De Morgan
        NOT     R2,R2
        NOT     R3,R3
        AND     R0,R2,R3
        NOT     R0,R0            ; a XOR b

        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     Restore2Xor
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
Restore2Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
Restore1Xor
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
ExitOpXor
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXor
        RET
SaveR7OpXor .FILL x0000

; OpDisplay -- Display top of stack (without popping)
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,SaveR7Disp
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     ExitOpDisplay
        ST      R0,SaveR0Disp   ; Save value
        JSR     BinarytoASCII
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUF
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap3
        LD      R0,SaveR0Disp
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Push displayed value back
        STR     R0,R6,#0
ExitOpDisplay
        LD      R7,SaveR7Disp
        RET
SaveR7Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR0Disp  .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap3 .FILL x0000
NewlineChar .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck -- Check if R0 is in [-999, 999]
;   R5=0 success, R5=1 failure. Preserves R0.
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,SaveR7RC
        ST      R0,SaveR0RC
        LD      R5,Neg999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 - 999
        BRp     BadRange
        LD      R5,Pos999
        ADD     R4,R0,R5       ; R4 = R0 + 999
        BRn     BadRange
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        RET
BadRange
        LEA     R0,RangeErrorMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap4
        LD      R0,SaveR0RC
        LD      R7,SaveR7RC
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
SaveR7RC     .FILL x0000
SaveR0RC     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap4  .FILL x0000
Neg999       .FILL  #-999
Pos999       .FILL  #999
RangeErrorMsg .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Error: Number is out of range."

; ============================================================
; PUSH and POP Subroutines (using fixed stack addresses)
; ============================================================

; POP: Pop top of stack into R0
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (underflow)
POP
        ST      R1,SaveR1Pop
        LD      R1,NegSBase      ; R1 = -x4000
        ADD     R1,R6,R1         ; R1 = R6 - x4000
        BRz     Underflow
        LDR     R0,R6,#0
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
Underflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Pop
        LD      R1,SaveR1Pop
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
SaveR1Pop    .FILL x0000
SaveR7Pop    .FILL x0000
NegSBase     .FILL xC000       ; -x4000
UnderflowMsg .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."

; PUSH: Push R0 onto the stack
;   R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure (overflow)
PUSH
        ST      R1,SaveR1Push
        LD      R1,NegSMax       ; R1 = -x3FF6
        ADD     R1,R6,R1         ; R1 = R6 - x3FF6
        BRz     Overflow
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        STR     R0,R6,#0
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
Overflow
        ST      R7,SaveR7Push
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Push
        LD      R1,SaveR1Push
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
SaveR1Push   .FILL x0000
SaveR7Push   .FILL x0000
NegSMax      .FILL xC00A       ; -x3FF6
OverflowMsg  .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."

        .END

turns.content:
One more: `OpDisplay` uses `LEA R0,ASCIIBUF` to print it with PUTS, but ASCIIBUF is now near PushValue (at ~x3088) while OpDisplay is at ~x31A9. The offset is -290, just slightly over the -256 limit.

I need to either move OpDisplay closer to ASCIIBUF, or use an indirect approach for loading the ASCIIBUF address. Let me restructure so OpDisplay is earlier in the file, before the operations that are further away. Actually, the simplest fix is to swap the order: put OpDisplay right after the ASCII conversion functions (near ASCIIBUF), and put the arithmetic ops after.

Let me reorganize the order of subroutines:

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"content": ";
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X (exit), C (clear), + (add), * (multiply),
;           % (mod), @ (XOR), neg/- (negate), D (display),
;           digits 0-9 (push integer)
;
; Stack: fixed addresses x3FFF (BASE) down to x3FF6 (MAX), 10 slots.
; R6 = stack pointer (points to top element, starts at x4000 = empty).
; R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure.
;

        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; Main Program
; ============================================================
        LD      R6,StackBaseVal  ; R6 = x4000 (empty stack)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        ST      R7,SaveR7Main
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Main

; ---- Check the command character in R0 ----
Test
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     Exit

        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpAdd

        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMult

        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpNeg

        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMod

        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpXor

        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpDisplay

        ; Otherwise must be a digit
        BRnzp   PushValue

; ---- Dispatch trampolines ----
DoOpAdd
        JSR     OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMult
        JSR     OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpNeg
        JSR     OpNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMod
        JSR     OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpXor
        JSR     OpXor
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpDisplay
        JSR     OpDisplay
        BRnzp   NewCommand

; ---- OpClear: reset stack pointer ----
OpClear
        LD      R6,StackBaseVal
        BRnzp   NewCommand

SaveR7Main   .FILL  x0000
StackBaseVal .FILL  x4000       ; Empty stack pointer value

PromptMsg   .FILL   x000A
            .STRINGZ "Enter a command:"

NegC        .FILL   xFFBD       ; -'C'
NegPlus     .FILL   xFFD5       ; -'+'
NegMinus    .FILL   xFFD3       ; -'-'

NegD        .FILL   xFFBC       ; -'D'
NegMod      .FILL   xFFDB       ; -'%'
NegAt       .FILL   xFFC0       ; -'@'

; ============================================================
; PushValue -- Read digits, convert to binary, push
; ============================================================

        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUF     ; R1 points to digit buffer

ValueLoop

        BRz     GoodInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     TooLargeInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        BRnzp   ValueLoop

GoodInput
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUF
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2        ; R1 = number of characters
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10

        LEA     R0,TooManyDigits
        ST      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Trap5
        LD      R7,SaveR7PV

SaveR7PV     .FILL x0000
SaveR7Trap5  .FILL x0000
TooManyDigits .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Too many digits"
MaxDigits    .FILL  x0003

; ---- Shared ASCII buffer (near PushValue, ASCIItoBinary, BinarytoASCII, OpDisplay) ----

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary -- Convert ASCII string to binary
;   R1 = number of digits, ASCIIBUF holds the digits
;   Result returned in R0
; ============================================================

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1

DoneAtoB

LookUp10    .FILL  #0
            .FILL  #10
            .FILL  #20
            .FILL  #30
            .FILL  #40
            .FILL  #50
            .FILL  #60
            .FILL  #70

Pos100      .FILL  x0064
Neg10       .FILL  xFFF6

SaveR0Disp  .FILL x0000

Restore2Add

Restore1Add

PosMultiplier

Restore2Mult

Restore1Mult

ExitOpMult
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult

SaveR7OpMult .FILL x0000

OpNeg   ST      R7,SaveR7OpNeg

ModNoSign

The program looks correct. Let me verify a few key negative ASCII values:

- `'X'` = 0x58, `-'X'` = 0xFFA8 ✓
- `'C'` = 0x43, `-'C'` = 0xFFBD ✓
- `'+'` = 0x2B, `-'+'` = 0xFFD5 ✓
- `'-'` = 0x2D, `-'-'` = 0xFFD3 ✓
- `'*'` = 0x2A, `-'*'` = 0xFFD6 ✓
- `'D'` = 0x44, `-'D'` = 0xFFBC ✓
- `'%'` = 0x25, `-'%'` = 0xFFDB ✓
- `'@'` = 0x40, `-'@'` = 0xFFC0 ✓

Let me also verify the range check logic:
- `LD R5,Neg999` → R5 = -999
- `ADD R4,R0,R5` → R4 = R0 + (-999) = R0 - 999
